notes:
Frankie lives in Rye. She lost her husband 18 months ago and now lives with her 5-year-old Cavoodle, Buddy. She is booked to visit the village on Thursday 26th at 11:30am to inspect Units 23 and 42. Mike to send the info pack with invite

Update: Frankie coming in on Friday 27th at 11:30am

display_name:
Matt Tait
(Enquiry on behalf of his parents)
notes:
Matt sent an enquiry on the BG website: I am looking for my parents for a retirement village. They have lived in Blackburn North for the last 55 years, where they still currently reside so staying local is important to them.
I am interested in how the costing is calculated at this village. If you could please send me some material or give me a call to discuss.
Update Sam: This is a strong prospect. I spoke with Matt and he’s keen to move quickly. I explained our DMF structure and he responded very positively, saying our offering is considerably stronger than others he has looked at.
His parents are both 84 and he feels they should have started this process five years ago. They are specifically interested in a 2-bedroom plus study configuration. Matt has reviewed our website and is particularly interested in Unit 42, and would also like to inspect a renovated unit.
He’ll is travelling in regional Victoria for the rest of this week but will be back Monday/Tuesday next week before heading to Sydney again for the remainder. He’s planning to speak with his parents to see if they can come in on Monday/Tuesday next week. Please encourage him to arrange an inspection then if possible.
Information pack and invitation to visit sent via email

Noel currently lives on 24acre land near Mansfield, age 79 and wants to downsize. Lost his wife 3 years ago after fighting cancer for 15 years so he is alone. Has one son in NSW and another in Geelong. Information pack has been sent and open invitation to visit village.

Chris enquired via direct email to sales@barrinagardens.com.au. Advised he is enquiring on behalf of his FIL who is looking to relocate to melbourne in 12-24 months. Information pack has been sent and open invitation to visit village.

Enquiry via our website. Site visit on 09/02/2026. Inspected Units 23 and 42. Preferred the size of Unit 42 over 23. We are their first retirement village. They are from Eltham. They have plans to visit other retirement villages.

May visited on 05/02 and inspected U33, 42 and 23. She lives in a 3 bedroom unit in Mount Waverly. Has stopped using emails (due to hacking) and requested we call on home phone number (has an answering machine). 

She wants to go on the waitlist for the 2 bedroom with garage style configuration unit. Please call her ASAP if one comes on the market.

She has looked into retirement living previously and is across loan lease, DMF and service fees.

Live in Blackburn South. Village tour organised for Tuesday 03/02 at 11am

Update: Helen and Ben visited and inspected Unit 42. Helen like the internal configuration of the unit but are seeking a fully renovated unit. I showed them Unit 24 to get a feel for kitchen/joinery finish, flooring, paint, tile etc. Helen really liked Unit 24. She mentioned that style of unit is a good fit for them as only Ben drives but he doesn’t drive much anymore. Ben didn’t do a lot of talking and seemed a little uninterested.

They can definitely afford a fully renovated Unit 42. They (Helen) is concerned about the impact if Ben had to go into higher care and the costs to send him there + DMF. With that on her mind the village / community benefits resonates with her. More conversation about the financials would likely help with the sale.

Overall they liked what they saw but I feel are undecided if they want to consider retirement living until they fully understand the financial impact. 

Update: Spoke to Helen on 12/02. They are still very confused about whether to proceed or not. They are going away on holiday next month. They are worried about losing Unit 42 but also not 100% comfortable with proceeding.

U42 prospect. Marjorie and Geoff are a Warrnambool-based couple looking to purchase a new home. Their daughter, Beth, who lives in Melbourne and works in palliative care, will be the key influencer in the decision; she plans to inspect properties first and advise her parents on suitability. The family is active in their local church and community.

Their budget is firmly set within the $700k–$800k range, aligning with the value of their current home. The next step is for Marjorie to coordinate with Geoff and Beth, after which she will call back to schedule a suitable time for a viewing.

Margaret is 82, a widow, and has lived in Heathmont for 40 years. Her address is 4 Locksley Close, Heathmont VIC 3135. We are the first retirement village she has contacted and she has requested an information pack to be sent by mail. She has two daughters and will discuss the idea with them before taking next steps. Very early stage, no follow-up call required at this time.

Peter lives in Ringwood and is just starting his journey. He’s interested in inspecting Unit 42 and receiving more information from us. Mike to send an intro email and invitation to visit and he will let us know when he can come
Requested information pack. Katherine has sent. Sam has left a voice mail and sent text asking her to call

Sue lives at 9 Banksia St, Blackburn. Looking at 2 Br with a garage or carport.
Sam spoke to Sue. Asked to go on waitlist for 2 bedroom with garage or carport (prefer renovated unit)

Sam 19/01: Pat is from meta. She inspected u42 today and liked the village and the unit, only complaint was the bedrooms are small. Pat is inspecting Cameron Close Retirement Village in Burwood on Wed 28th at 10:00am to compare with BG as she hasn’t viewed any other villages yet.  I committed to a follow-up call after her inspection. She also asked to be notified if anyone lodges an EOI for Unit 42.
No answer, did not leave message

No answer

Previously spent 21 years in a retirement village in Phillip Island; left due to a poor experience with prior management. Looking for a 2-bedroom unit. Discussed Unit 23. Daughter lives in Ringwood. Son-in-law is unwell (pacemaker following seizures/collapses), reinforcing desire to be close to family. Very positive about Barrina Gardens and keen. They will contact us when next in Melbourne to arrange an inspection.

Judy (lives in Davenport, Tasmania). She inspected Unit 42 on 3rd January 2026. She is seeking either a renovated 2 bedroom with garage or 2 bedroom plus study with garage. Robyn has requested we add them to a waitlist for either of these style of units. Contact in 2 months requested

06/01:  I’ve just spoken with Wendy (details below). She’s very interested in Barrina Gardens, although she and her husband are likely about 12 to 18 months away from relocating. They currently live in Templestowe and have done quite a bit of research, and it sounds like our village is exactly what they’re looking for.

Wendy would like to come in for a tour of a two-bed plus study unit to confirm it will suit them when they’re ready to move. She was also quite keen to meet me personally once she found out I’m the owner so it would be best to coordinate a visit around the time of the upcoming committee meeting in February,

Email sent - requested if 10:30am or 2pm on Wednesday 11th February suits her for site visit with Sam
Sam 18/01: Followed up to see if she can make the appointment on 11th Feb. She said she will check her mail and respond

Update: Kath is very interested. Her preference is Unit 38. Will be meeting with agent to get a valuation on her property. Is seeking a full unit renovation.
Sam 05/12: Kath has signed with an agent she knows, and the property will go online from 24 January. Auction is set for 28 February. She said she will call me back next week to complete the EOI. BOOM!
Sam 8/12: Received signed EOI for 800k with 100k reno. Awaiting approval from the family on the offer

Derek is assisting his mum Lisa who is at early stages of looking at retirement living. We discussed units 33 and 42 and upcoming units. We discussed information about body corporate, freehold purchase and process for exit (including DMF). Have sent email and look to schedule time for Lisa and Derek to visit village.
06/10: Derek has reviewed information sent and has requested date Unit 33 will be available to inspect
Inspection booked for 17th October

He will discuss with Barb but they are definitely keen to move to Barrina
Sam 21/10 - Bert put an enquiry online to see what we have on offer. Painting is being done and floors need to be sanded in current home to prepare for sale. Suggested he come and inspect unit 33. Follow up if he does not contact us
Sam 05/11 - Bert and Barbara are coming in for an inspection tomorrow @ 10am. Their current address is 9 Somerset Court, Blackburn South, and they expect a sale price between $1.3M–$1.4M. Potential Units: 33 or 42. Bert mentioned needing assistance with tradies to prepare his home for sale. I advised him to inspect first, and if they decide to proceed, he can then consult his agent for advice on whether any flooring or cosmetic updates are necessary. Dee lives in Vermont and is preparing her house for sale to move to village. She has seen https://centennialliving.com.au/village/homes-for-sale/cameron-close-village/ and love it. She inspected Barrina Wednesday 29th @ 11am.
Sam 29/10: Dee is actively inspecting several villages and is focused on maximising her cash position to contribute more to her super. She’s visiting multiple villages this week and next, and wants to view a renovated unit at Barrina. Mike to advise when unit 24 reno is complete as she would like to see it.  Her property in Vermont is scheduled for auction February 28th.
Sam 11/12: Spoke with Dee. She wants to see the Unit 24 renovation and confirmed she needs a garage, so Unit 42 is the only suitable option. Her budget is $600–700k, but needs a newer unit. If we had a 2-bed with a garage available, she would take it immediately. Call her if one becomes available. She is not making any decisions yet until her home sells then she will make a decision.
Mike 13/02: Dee inspected renovated 2 bedroom Unit 24 and Unit 23 (under renovation). Likes the finish and colour schemes. Confirms she wants to be on waitlist for 2 Bedroom with Garage but budget needs to be under $700k and wants fully renovated unit.

Sam and Barbra, from Malvern East, plan to leave their home to their grandchildren and move into the village. They’ve been overwhelmed with inquiries from Facebook, but after speaking with them, I believe we’re now their top choice. They have an SMSF and can comfortably afford Unit 42 at $730K. The couple travel frequently overseas for humanitarian aid work and are devoted Christians. They are booked for a tour on Monday, 27th at 11:30 am, and when they inspect the village, it would be ideal to introduce them to Beryl and Kanna.
Address: G7/1399 Dandenong Road Malvern East, VIC 3145

Sam 28/10: Sam and Barbra are interested in a renovated unit 42. Mike to put together quotation for reno and send to Sam.
Sam has had 3x agents come through their apartment, they just need to select an agent. He is also considering https://mymoneymyhome.com.au/ who offer a buy it now price. Sam Elsha to follow up with Sam
Sam 03/11 - I advised Sam that the My Money, My Home did not contact me regarding the enquiry l made online. Sam is now going to talk to local agents about selling his home. He said to give him a couple more days and he will call me. Put a call back in 1 week
Sam 11/12: His budget is no more than $700k for a renovated unit. Call him back if one becomes available.

Richard called seeking information about BG.  I sent him the assorted info and we talked about his requirements.  He is looking for a 2 1/2 with garage.  He said they would have a browse thru the info and then call to arrange a visit.

Sam 27/10: Spoke with Richard. He and his wife, Hui, are definitely interested in downsizing and are currently deciding which village best suits them. They drove through our village one Saturday, spoke with some residents, and later contacted our office.
They live in South Frankston in a four-bedroom home, which they believe will sell quickly. Richard will discuss timing with Hui and get back to us to arrange an inspection.
I mentioned the upcoming renovation for Unit 24, so showing photos of our previous work and arranging inspections of Units 42 and 33 would be ideal. Follow up in one week if we haven’t heard back.
Sam 05/11 - No answer, did not leave message
Sam 11/12 - Richard would like to tour our village but has not time between now and Christmas. We agreed to call him early in Jan to work out a time for an inspection

Sam 21/10: Nick, 82, and Marie, 77, live in Hoppers Crossing and want to relocate closer to family on this side of town. Nick is facing cancer for the second time and recently learned he’ll soon be a great-grandfather. They previously paid a deposit for a village eight years ago, but Nick changed his mind at the last minute. Marie plans to speak with him and try to convince him to attend an inspection next Tuesday when they’re back in the area.
Sam 29/10: Marie cancelled the inspection as Mick was readmitted to hospital for possible surgery. She’s unable to move at this stage. I sent a text message checking in on Mick’s health.
Sam 12/12: Sent a text message to ask how Mick is doing now

Sam 27/10: Spoke with Emmy. He has sold his house in Campbellfield for $803k and wants to move closer to his daughter in Bentleigh and his partner in Hampton. He’s concerned about the village location being a bit far but liked what I explained about it. He’s interested in Unit 33 and possibly the renovated Unit 24.
Mike to send an introductory pack and ask for a suitable date and time for Emmy and his daughter (who is assisting him with everything) to come for an inspection.
Sam 27/10: Gordanka husband just died, she asked to call me back when she is ready, so sad. Call her back in 1 week to see how she is.

Sam 27/10: Spoke with Raphael. He’s Sri Lankan and heading to Sri Lanka in November to spend Christmas there. He plans to sell his home in Pakenham mid-January after returning.
He’s interested in a one-bedroom unit (Unit 6) — quoted $395k unrenovated or $440k renovated.
Mike to send an introductory pack and inspection invitation for Tuesday, 28th October at 2:00 pm
Sam 28/10: Raph attended an inspection and viewed Units 7 and 23. Later that afternoon, Sam spoke with him and confirmed his budget is $300–350k. Sam suggested that Raph get in touch after returning from Sri Lanka following Christmas to review available options, with Unit 58 mentioned as a possible opportunity @ 395k.

Sam 27/10: Spoke with Faye and her husband, Clive. They currently live in Warrnambool and are planning to relocate to Melbourne. They’ve already inspected units in Hampton and Vermont and are interested in a two-bedroom option.
I mentioned Unit 24, which will be fully renovated and priced around $550k — they were very interested. I also explained the parking arrangements.
They’re booked to inspect on Saturday, 1st November at 1:30 pm.
Mike to send an introductory email and inspection invitation.

Inspection was cancelled by Faye

Sam 29/10: Spoke with Robert. He and his partner are deciding between purchasing a small townhouse or moving into a retirement village. They requested an information brochure to be mailed to 25 Nicholas Grove, Heatherton VIC 3202. Please send both a physical info pack and a digital copy via email, then follow up in approximately two weeks.

Marie is from Hobart.  She is interested in a 2 br plus study.  She is looking to move to Melb.  And is very interested in Units 42 (which I showed her) and 38 when it comes on the market.  She saw BG on real estate website.  She has a son who wishes to buy her place in Sandy Bay, Hobart, but will need to visit here to attend his father (her ex) who is in care.  I have given her a folder and will follow up when 38 is available.
Sam 09/10 – Marie liked Barrina but is currently more interested in a regular property she found in Ringwood (4/22 Thomas Street, Ringwood VIC 3134). She’s flying back from Tasmania specifically to inspect it. She mentioned that if she decides to go with a village, Barrina will be her choice. She’ll get in touch if she wishes to proceed further.

Sent Email 13/10
Sam 27/10: Left a voice message to call and also sent a message via Meta.

Sam 20/10: David is interested in 2bed with garage suggested unit 33, sent intro pack with invite to come and inspect, follow up if we don’t hear back.

His wife just had hip operation, looking to downsize, want to see what we offer.

Sandra is 68, living with her daughter and raising her grandchildren but now wants her independence. Sam quoted 400k for a renovated 1 bed. She has money tied in investments but can make it work. Her daughter is having her second child in March and she wants to be out before hand.
Enquiry from meta marketing. Intro pack sent 18/09/2025

Sam 16/09: Julie enquired about Unit 42. She is 57, has MS, and is very interested in a strata title village. I provided her with an overview of the village, and she said she would call back in a couple of days with further questions. Julie has been closely tracking our units and noted that a similar one went under offer within 3 weeks, which prompted her to call quickly when she saw this listing. If she doesn’t call, I recommend following up with her in 1 week.

Linda is interested in a 2-bedroom unit with a garage and is planning to move in approximately 12 months, depending on her daughter and husband relocating nearby. She has lived in Eltham for the past 10 years but knows the area well. Linda has a passion for floral work and used to visit Barrina around 15 years ago as part of a floral group. I suggest we keep in touch with her each quarter.

display_name:
George (Son) enquiring for:
Bill (Dad) and Melanie (Mum)
notes:
George found our listing for Unit 36 on REA. He is assisting Mum and Dad who live in Box Hill and are looking to put the house up on the market. We had a chat about the village and what we have to offer. He was asking about No Agent property (he was curious as Mum and Dad's house is about to go on the market). Email sent 29/07

Sam 11/08: Left a message for George to call me

Spoke to Margi about Barrina. She had a lot of questions about how it all worked. Answered her questions and offered to send her email with further information. She wasn't wanting to commit to visiting us but will follow up early next week to see if we can make an appointment

Jill came in off the street.  She had seen our website and was interested in seeing unit 11, and 36.  She is currently living in Heathmont and has a son who lives in Croydon.
She is interested in retirement villages and looking at options. She doesn’t seem to be in a rush and it seems like she has just started looking. She liked the units but didn't give much feedback. She was impressed at how low our service fee was. She thought the community hall was a bit tired.
Sam 16/07: No answer, did not leave a message

Bronwyn and Myra visited the village on Friday 16th at 10am. Bronwyn lives nearby on Canterbury Road. Myra lives in a 2 bedroom + study unit in Box Hill (not retirement living). She moved to the unit with her husband (downsized) but finds she does not use most of the unit (quite large) since her husband passed away. Myra advised she is feeling a little lonely. She saw our village advertisement on Facebook and remembered us as she has walked through here with her granddaughter. She spoke to her daughter (Bronwyn) about this so Bronwyn arranged a visit (advised just looking for now).

Bronwyn and Myra advised preference would be a 2 bedroom. We looked at Units 11 and 36 and Myra was quite impressed with Unit 11. Before leaving we looked at Unit 7 and she was very impressed and likes the look and feel of both 7 and 11. She wants to think about it - feels quite genuine and I think very interested in both options.
Update 21/05: Bronwyn advised that its been a bit of a rollercoaster for Myra and she is in 2 minds whether to proceed or not. She didn't indicate they were viewing any other villages. I suggested that perhaps they visit again so we can look at Unit 7 and 11. She was going to talk to Myra about it.
Sam 27/05: Not ready to move yet. Follow up in 3 months

Joanne contacted the office and requested to view Unit 36 at 1pm Friday 7th March. 

They are both in their 80s, originally from the UK, and currently live at 23 Maroong Drive, Research VIC 3095. Their home is worth approximately $1.5M, and they’re keen to downsize. They’ve already engaged multiple agents and about to select one.
Sam 07/03: Joey and Ian inspected u11 & u36. They liked both units and were going to go away and discuss which unit they preferred. Follow up with them daily
Sam 10/04: I sent a text to Joey advising u36 is now officially for sale and suggested another inspection. 
Sam 22/04: Sent text to invite for second inspection
Sam 16/07: No answer, did not leave a message

Geoff visited the village on the 15th Jan as a walk in. He is 87 you and has a 4 bedroom home in Nunawading. His wife recently passed away and he is looking at retirement living. He found out about us via a former friend he played golf with who lives at Pavilions. Geoff is interested in either Unit 7 or 11. His son Andrew has been helping him and he is hoping to get Andrew's opinion. Will be arranging another site visit with Andrew. Call back on Friday 17/01 to confirm date.
Update 17/01: spoke to Geoff to confirm appointment to see units with Andrew on the 18/01. Geoff confirmed he is still interested in Barrina Gardens but wants to put off visiting with Andrew. He kept referring to a number of other matters he has to complete first prior to taking any further steps. I explained to him the EOI process again as I mentioned 7 and 11 will likely go if we don't secure them asap. Geoff didn't want to proceed. Set callback for early February 2025
Update 03/02 - could not reach Geoff by phone. Try again in 7 days.
Sam 12/03: Had a lengthy conversation with Geoff, who currently lives at 9 Koala Ave, Nunawading VIC 3131. He has two sons—Richard (based in South Melbourne, 0411 603 941) and another Richard, who is in the Army—as well as a daughter, Kerin, who lives in Sydney.
Geoff's cognitive sharpness is declining, and he feels he needs intellectually engaging conversations. Initially, he didn’t understand that we are a retirement living community, but after multiple explanations, he eventually grasped the concept. Once he did, he really liked the village.
He is hesitant to leave his home of 56 years, believing he can continue living independently for another 12 to 24 months. However, Andrew is encouraging him to move sooner. I will follow up with Andrew to get his perspective.
Sam 10/04: Spoke with Andrew (son)at length. He wants his dad to move into a village. He asked me to call him back once I return from Alaska so he can update me on his dad’s situation. At the moment, his dad has been advised that he can receive care services at home; however, Andrew remains unconvinced. He would like some time to pass before reassessing the situation with his dad and me.

Catherine (Cathy) found our listing for Unit 11 on REA and contacted us for a same-day inspection.

She visited the village and really liked Unit 11 but wants to discuss it with her family before making a decision. She currently owns a house in Mulgrave, and her purchase would be subject to the sale of her existing property. Previously, she had a family home in Keysborough before downsizing to a three-bedroom in Mulgrave. Now, she is looking for something smaller and is particularly interested in the community aspect of village living.

One of her sons works in the CBD Monday to Friday and stays with her on weekends. She asked if this would be an issue. I explained that visitors are welcome, but they cannot occupy visitor parking and would need to park on Barrina Street. If he needed to stay for an extended period, it would require office approval. I also made it clear that a family member cannot live with her permanently. She understands.
Sam 12/03: No answer, did not leave a message
Sam 22/04: No answer, did not leave a message

Ross (Son) and Judy (Mother) visited the village on the 25th November and inspected Unit 7 and Unit 11.
